content.full_text:
July 14, 1992

Scott Hallabrin, General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

Subject: Determination of conflict of interest

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

Earlier this week I spoke with Mr. John Wallace who advised me of the factors to be considered in determining whether I may be confronted with a conflict on interest as a result of making a certain investment in the future. Please provide me with an opinion as to whether the following circumstances would result in a conflict of interest.

I am a Senior Planner working for the Lassen County Planning Department. One area of my responsibility is in economic development. A major possible project in economic development would be the development of a major ski area and accompanying amenities in the Westwood area of Lassen County. Westwood is located within the Almanor Basin which lies primarily within the County of Plumas. It is anticipated that property values in the Almanor Basin could be affected if the ski area is developed.

My parents, adult brothers, their wives, my wife and I are contemplating the purchase of a vacation cabin or house in the Lake Almanor area. The nearest Lake Almanor development is approximately five road-miles from the base area of the possible ski area and the furthest development might be as far as thirty road-miles. All developed areas are within the County of Plumas. We have not been seriously shopping as of the date of this letter so I cannot be specific as to the location or the setting of the investment. However, the typical setting would be that of a rural, lake-oriented neighborhood with parcels averaging one-quarter to one acre in area and being owned by individuals who use the cabins for summer retreats or for rental income. It is anticipated that in any location there would be dozens of individual landowners within 2500 feet of any site that we might choose.

Additionally, please consider that our plans may involve the purchase of a vacant building site and the construction of a cabin as an alternative to purchasing a house. We may also rent the cabin on an occasional basis through one of the many rental property management firms operating in the Almanor Basin.

If you require further information, please do not hesitate to contact me.

Sincerely,

Joseph J. Bertotti,
Senior Planner
